title: Causal latent tire-state features from a state-space filter
summary: A recent F1 tire-degradation paper models stint pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows it beats an AR(1) benchmark across rolling-origin race validation.
rationale: A recent F1 tire-degradation paper models stint pace as a latent state with pit-stop resets and shows it beats an AR(1) benchmark across rolling-origin race validation. Your current features use observed deltas and ratios, but not an explicit latent degradation level, slope, innovation shock, or reset strength.
implementation_hint: Within each CV fold, fit a simple causal local-linear-trend/Kalman-style model per driver-stint using only past laps; emit fold-safe features such as latent tire pace, latent degradation slope, one-step-ahead residual, post-pit reset magnitude, and compound-specific slope. Feed those features into the existing tree stack; do not fit the filter on combined train+test.
expected_effect: Low-correlation signal that should help especially on long stints, noisy laps, and compound transitions where observed lap-time deltas are unstable.
risk: Easy to leak future information if smoothing is bidirectional or fit on full stints. A full Bayesian fit may be too slow; start with a lightweight causal filter.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
